author: Bombardelli
text: Error on trying to run detectron2 on Mac M1 with device set to "mps" (Metal Performance Shaders)

author: Bombardelli
text:
### 🐛 Describe the bug

I am trying to run detectron2 on Mac M1 with device set to "mps" (Metal Performance Shaders)

The problem is that every time I set up to mps the following error shows up:

Error: command buffer exited with error status.  
The Metal Performance Shaders operations encoded on it may not have completed.  
Error:  
(null)  
Internal Error (0000000e:Internal Error)  
<AGXG13GFamilyCommandBuffer: 0x28bad31a0>  
label =   
device = <AGXG13GDevice: 0x14ed4ca00>  
name = Apple M1  
commandQueue = <AGXG13GFamilyCommandQueue: 0x14fded000>  
label =   
device = <AGXG13GDevice: 0x14ed4ca00>  
name = Apple M1  
retainedReferences = 1

Anyone else with the same problem? I believe there is currently an incompatibility of "mps" with detectron2. Because when you set to "cpu" it works normally, but it is cpu and therefore slower for training.

```

        name = Apple M1 Max 
    commandQueue = <AGXG13XFamilyCommandQueue: 0x130cc4400>
        label = <none> 
        device = <AGXG13XDevice: 0x12566b200>
            name = Apple M1 Max 
    retainedReferences = 1
```

```
numpy                                        1.24.4
torch                                        2.2.0
torchaudio                                   2.2.0
torchvision                                  0.17.0
```

Hello,

> TL;DR: It is OOM  
> [jax-ml/jax#19110 (comment)](https://github.com/jax-ml/jax/issues/19110#issuecomment-1868385445)

I had same issue on my project. but it becomes to work when I cut down `batch size`  
literally, I searched the line `Error: command buffer exited with error status.` around every google, github, pytorch forum  
they commonly say that reducing batch size was work in some how. VRAM issue is the main reason I suspect. no one knew the actual reason but their experience have suffcient grounds.

hope this would help

text:

author: Noah-Yarmush
text:
I have a similar error for **device = "mps"** and training **torchvision.models.detection.fasterrcnn\_resnet50\_fpn** network. However, when I hardly code **device = "cpu"** it performs fast (few seconds) and there's no error message but I cannot say at this moment if the training goes successfully and is not stopped internally without errors.

I use MacBook Air M1 8GB RAM 256GB HDD Sonoma 14.5, script of python run from terminal.

author: Kacper0199
text:
I am encountering the same issue while running `fasterrcnn_resnet50_fpn` with `FastRCNNPredictor` on my MacBook M1 Pro using the "mps" device (Metal Performance Shaders). The error appears multiple times during training with the following message:

```
Error: command buffer exited with error status.
The Metal Performance Shaders operations encoded on it may not have completed.
Error:
(null)
Internal Error (0000000e:Internal Error)
<AGXG13XFamilyCommandBuffer: 0x4baef3030>
label = <none>
device = <AGXG13XDevice: 0x30c52b600>
name = Apple M1 Pro
commandQueue = <AGXG13XFamilyCommandQueue: 0x12f870800>
label = <none>
device = <AGXG13XDevice: 0x30c52b600>
name = Apple M1 Pro
retainedReferences = 1
```

I am using the following setup to train a Faster R-CNN model with 2 classes on 800px images:

```
def get_model(num_classes):
    model = torchvision.models.detection.fasterrcnn_resnet50_fpn(weights='DEFAULT')
    in_features = model.roi_heads.box_predictor.cls_score.in_features
    model.roi_heads.box_predictor = FastRCNNPredictor(in_features, num_classes)
    return model

num_classes = 2
model = get_model(num_classes)
model.to(device)
```

The error occurs at random points during training, typically after a few iterations, and sometimes freezes the system.

Here is my environment:

```
PyTorch: 2.4.1
Torchvision: 0.19.1
NumPy: 1.26.4
Device: MacBook M1 Pro
Batch size: 4 and more
```

Like others, I found that training on cpu works, but it’s slower. Reducing the batch size also delays the issue but doesn’t fully resolve it, slowing training process. I believe there is still some incompatibility with mps and fasterrcnn\_resnet50\_fpn ...

Has anyone found a reliable workaround or fix for this issue?
